title: Kareng Ghar
tagline: Kareng Ghar — heritage city of Assam

overview.short: Kareng (Assamese: [kɑ:ɹɛŋ ˈgɑ:], "royal palace"), also known as The Garhgaon Palace, is located in Garhgaon 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Sivasagar, Assam, India.
overview.description: Kareng (Assamese: [kɑ:ɹɛŋ ˈgɑ:], "royal palace"), also known as The Garhgaon Palace, is located in Garhgaon 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Sivasagar, Assam, India. The palace structures were made of wood and stone. In 1751 Sunenphaa, son of Sukhrungphaa, constructed the brick wall of about 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) in length surrounding the Garhgaon Palace and the masonry gate leading to it. After the destruction of the old palace it was rebuilt around 1752 as the present seven-storied structure by Suremphaa (1751–1769).

topPlaces.name: Garhgaon
topPlaces.description: Gargaon (Tai-Ahom:Tsé-hung;pronounced [ɡaɹˈɡãʊ]) is a historic city in Assam, India and served as the capital of the Ahom kingdom for many years. It was built by the Ahom king Suklenmung (Gargaiya Roja) in 1540.

topPlaces.name: Ahom kingdom
topPlaces.description: The Ahom kingdom (), or Kingdom of Assam, was a late medieval kingdom in the Brahmaputra Valley. It is conventionally dated from 1228 to 1826, although the chronology of its foundation is disputed.

topPlaces.name: Simaluguri
topPlaces.description: Gargaon is a town area in Sivasagar district in the state of Assam, India.The Dikhow River flows near it.

topPlaces.name: Bhogamukh
topPlaces.description: Bhogamukh is located in Assam, India. It comes under two districts Jorhat and Sivasagar.

items.note: Gargaon (Tai-Ahom:Tsé-hung;pronounced [ɡaɹˈɡãʊ]) is a historic city in Assam, India and served as the capital of the Ahom kingdom for many years. It was built by the Ahom king Suklenmung (Gargaiya Roja) in 1540.
The Ahom kingdom (), or Kingdom of Assam, was a late medieval kingdom in the Brahmaputra Valley. It is conventionally dated from 1228 to 1826, although the chronology of its foundation is disputed.
Gargaon is a town area in Sivasagar district in the state of Assam, India.The Dikhow River flows near it.
Bhogamukh is located in Assam, India. It comes under two districts Jorhat and Sivasagar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kareng Ghar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kareng Ghar is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kareng Ghar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jorhat Airport (61 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (153 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kareng Ghar?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kareng Ghar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kareng Ghar?
faq.a: Stays in Kareng Ghar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kareng Ghar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Garhgaon, Ahom kingdom, Simaluguri, Bhogamukh.
